title: File changes from macos host aren't notified to podman container
body: ### Issue Description File change events (macos) aren't propagated to podman container ### Steps to reproduce the issue 1. Create a podman container with shared directory using `podman run -it --rm -v $(pwd):/shared_directory alpine:latest` 2. Install inotify tools and add listener on **hello.txt** using ```bash #!/bin/bash apk add bash inotify-tools bash file="/shared_directory/hello.txt" inotifywait -m -e modify "$file" | while read -r directory event filename; do if [ "$event" = "MODIFY" ]; then echo "File $file Modified" fi done ``` 3. Update the file from hostos (macos) using `echo Hello >> hello.txt` ### Describe the results you received - Files changes aren't notified on podman container. ### Describe the results you expected - Expected following output ```bash File /shared_directory/hello.txt Modified File /shared_directory/hello.txt Modified ``` Replicated the same on docker, and file changes events are visible there - Docker steps ```bash docker run -it --rm -v $(pwd):/shared_directory alpine:latest #!/bin/bash apk add bash inotify-tools bash file="/shared_directory/hello.txt" inotifywait -m -e modify "$file" | while read -r directory event filename; do if [ "$event" = "MODIFY" ]; then echo "File $file Modified" fi done ``` ### podman info output ```yaml - `podman -v` `podman version 5.0.2` - OS: Macos `sw_vers` ProductName: macOS ProductVersion: 14.4.1 BuildVersion: 23E224 ``` ### Podman in a container No ### Privileged Or Rootless None ### Upstream Latest Release Yes ### Additional environment details N/A ### Additional information _No response_
